name: Kareo
skip_reason: Kareo has been rebranded/merged into Tebra. The official kareo.com domain now 301-redirects to tebra.com, and a customer testimonial on the Tebra homepage states "When Kareo became Tebra it just made my job easier," confirming the product no longer operates under the Kareo name.

name: Zocdoc
skip_reason: The official website (www.zocdoc.com and www.zocdoc.com/about) returned HTTP 403 Forbidden on repeated fetch attempts, so no verifiable first-party facts could be extracted from the site itself.

enrichment.short_description: Doctolib is an online platform for booking appointments with doctors and healthcare providers and offers video consultations.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Book doctor and healthcare appointments online with Doctolib, including video consultations, across France, Germany, and Italy.

features.title: Online appointment booking
features.description: Patients can search for healthcare providers and book available appointment slots directly online.

features.title: Video consultations
features.description: Telemedicine video consultations are available directly through the platform.

features.title: Specialist directory
features.description: Listings cover 30+ medical specialties, including general practitioners, dentists, pediatricians, gynecologists, dermatologists, and cardiologists.

features.title: Mobile apps
features.description: Dedicated iOS and Android apps let patients book and manage appointments on the go.

features.title: Health content and help center
features.description: The site provides help center resources and articles on health topics for patients.

pros: Covers 30+ medical specialties in a single directory
Available as a website plus dedicated iOS and Android apps
Offers video consultations alongside in-person appointment booking
B Corp certified
Operates across multiple European countries (France, Germany, Italy)
cons: Primarily serves French, German, and Italian markets, with limited availability elsewhere
Pricing for healthcare practitioners is not published on the site
No integrations were disclosed in the publicly available content

faqs.question: What is Doctolib used for?
faqs.answer: Doctolib lets patients search for and book appointments online with doctors and other healthcare providers, and it also offers video consultations.

faqs.question: Is Doctolib available outside of France?
faqs.answer: Yes. In addition to France, Doctolib operates in Germany and Italy.

faqs.question: Does Doctolib have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Doctolib offers apps for both iOS and Android.

faqs.question: Is Doctolib a certified company?
faqs.answer: Doctolib is B Corp certified.

use_cases.title: Booking a doctor's appointment
use_cases.description: Patients search by specialty and location, then book an available appointment slot online.

use_cases.title: Telemedicine consultations
use_cases.description: Patients and providers connect over video for remote visits booked through the platform.

use_cases.title: Finding specialists across multiple countries
use_cases.description: Providers list their availability so patients in France, Germany, and Italy can find and book care.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Doctolib?
paragraphs: Doctolib is an online platform that lets patients search for doctors and other healthcare providers and book appointments directly, with support for video consultations for telemedicine visits.
The directory spans 30+ medical specialties, including general practitioners, dentists, pediatricians, gynecologists, dermatologists, and cardiologists, and the company is B Corp certified.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Doctolib is for
paragraphs: Doctolib is built for patients in France, Germany, and Italy who want to find and book healthcare appointments online, as well as the healthcare practitioners who list their availability on the platform.

enrichment.short_description: Cliniko is practice management software for clinics and allied health practitioners covering scheduling, records, invoicing, and payments.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Cliniko practice management software: scheduling, treatment records, invoicing, and payments for clinics, priced from $45/month with a free trial.

features.title: Schedule management
features.description: Manage practitioner calendars and appointment scheduling for the clinic.

features.title: Treatment records
features.description: Document client treatment records within the platform.

features.title: Invoicing and payments
features.description: Create invoices and process payments directly in Cliniko.

features.title: Connected apps
features.description: Connects with third-party apps including MailChimp and Xero.

features.title: SMS reminders
features.description: Send SMS messages to clients at a cost of 10 cents per message.

features.title: 24/7 support
features.description: Includes unlimited free support via chat and email.

pros: Transparent, publicly listed pricing tiers based on practitioner count
30-day free trial available before committing to a paid plan
Unlimited support included via 24/7 chat and email
Donates 2% of subscription revenue to charity
Integrates with tools such as MailChimp and Xero
cons: SMS reminders are billed separately at 10 cents per message
Pricing scales by number of practitioners, which can add up for larger clinics
Specific mobile app and deeper integration details were not disclosed on the fetched pages

faqs.question: How much does Cliniko cost?
faqs.answer: Cliniko is priced by practitioner count, starting at $45/month for 1 practitioner and scaling up to $395/month for 26-200 practitioners.

faqs.question: Does Cliniko offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cliniko offers a 30-day free trial.

faqs.question: Does Cliniko charge for SMS reminders?
faqs.answer: Yes, SMS messages are billed at 10 cents per message.

faqs.question: What integrations does Cliniko support?
faqs.answer: Cliniko offers connected apps including MailChimp and Xero.

use_cases.title: Running a single-practitioner clinic
use_cases.description: A solo allied health practitioner manages scheduling, treatment records, and invoicing on the $45/month plan.

use_cases.title: Managing a multi-practitioner clinic
use_cases.description: Clinics with several practitioners use tiered plans to manage shared scheduling, billing, and client records.

use_cases.title: Sending appointment reminders
use_cases.description: Clinics use paid SMS messaging to remind clients of upcoming appointments.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Cliniko?
paragraphs: Cliniko is practice management software built for clinics and allied health practitioners, covering scheduling, treatment record documentation, invoicing, and payments.
Pricing is tiered by the number of practitioners, ranging from $45/month for a single practitioner up to $395/month for 26-200 practitioners, with a 30-day free trial available.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Cliniko is for
paragraphs: Cliniko is for allied health practitioners and clinic operators who need an integrated system for scheduling, client records, and billing, from solo practitioners to larger multi-practitioner clinics.

enrichment.short_description: Pabau is all-in-one practice management software for medical, aesthetic, and wellness clinics, covering scheduling, clinical, payments, and marketing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pabau practice management software for clinics: scheduling, clinical charting, payments, and marketing, with plans starting at £50/month.

features.title: Scheduling
features.description: Calendar, online booking, class booking, and waitlist management.

features.title: Clinical tools
features.description: Digital forms, charting, labs, prescriptions, telehealth, and before/after photos.

features.title: Payments and billing
features.description: Point-of-sale via Pabau Pay, invoicing, insurance billing, and quotes.

features.title: Operations management
features.description: Team management, commissions, timesheets, and inventory tracking.

features.title: Marketing tools
features.description: Email and SMS campaigns, lead management, loyalty programs, and referrals.

features.title: AI agents
features.description: Includes a scribe agent for clinical notes, a prescribing agent with safety checks, and a letters agent.

features.title: Pabau GO mobile app
features.description: A dedicated iPhone and iPad app for managing the practice on the go.

pros: Reports being used by 3,500+ practices worldwide
Full feature access on every plan, including scheduling, payments, and marketing automation
No per-patient fees on any pricing tier
Native integrations with tools such as HubSpot, Google Calendar, Xero, Stripe, Klarna, and Zapier
Dedicated iOS app (Pabau GO) in addition to the web platform
cons: Only the entry-level Starter plan has published pricing; other tiers require a custom quote
Founded year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site
No Android app was mentioned in the fetched content

faqs.question: How much does Pabau cost?
faqs.answer: The published Starter plan (1 user, up to 100 patients) is £50/month. Larger Solo, Team, Medium, Group, and Enterprise plans use custom pricing based on team size.

faqs.question: Does Pabau charge per patient?
faqs.answer: No, Pabau states there are no per-patient fees on any tier; pricing is based on the number of logged-in users.

faqs.question: What integrations does Pabau support?
faqs.answer: Pabau natively integrates with Healthcode, HubSpot, Google Calendar, Xero, Stripe, Klarna, WhatsApp, and Zapier, and supports open APIs and webhooks.

faqs.question: Does Pabau include AI tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pabau offers AI agents for clinical scribing, prescribing safety checks, and letter generation.

faqs.question: Is there a Pabau mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pabau GO is available for iPhone and iPad.

use_cases.title: Running a medical spa or aesthetic clinic
use_cases.description: Practices use scheduling, before/after photos, and Pabau Pay for point-of-sale transactions.

use_cases.title: Managing a multi-practitioner clinic
use_cases.description: Team management, commissions, and timesheets support clinics with several staff members.

use_cases.title: Automating patient marketing
use_cases.description: Clinics run email and SMS campaigns and manage loyalty and referral programs.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted clinical documentation
use_cases.description: Practitioners use the scribe and letters agents to speed up notes and correspondence.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pabau?
paragraphs: Pabau is all-in-one practice management software that combines clinical care, business operations, and patient engagement tools for medical, aesthetic, and wellness practices.
It reports being trusted by 3,500+ practices worldwide, with pricing starting at £50/month for a single-user Starter plan and custom pricing for larger teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pabau is for
paragraphs: Pabau serves medical spas, aesthetic clinics, dermatology, cosmetic surgery, physiotherapy, mental health, wellness clinics, GP practices, and fertility clinics, from solo practitioners to enterprises.

name: Power Diary
skip_reason: Power Diary rebranded to Zanda in November 2024. The powerdiary.com domain 301-redirects to zandahealth.com, and industry press (e.g. EIN Presswire: "Power Diary Rebrands to Zanda with a Bold Vision for Health Practice Management") confirms the product no longer operates under the Power Diary name, even though ownership and functionality carried over.

enrichment.short_description: Carepatron is practice management and EHR software with a free plan, AI scribe, telehealth, scheduling, and billing for health practitioners.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Carepatron practice management software: scheduling, telehealth, AI scribe, and billing for clinicians, with a free plan and paid plans from $31/month.

features.title: Online booking
features.description: Client booking with automatic appointment reminders.

features.title: Secure client messaging
features.description: SMS and email messaging with clients.

features.title: Telehealth video
features.description: Video sessions with in-session note-taking.

features.title: AI Scribe
features.description: Automated clinical documentation generated during sessions.

features.title: Treatment plan management
features.description: Tools for building and tracking client treatment plans.

features.title: ePrescribe
features.description: Electronic prescribing available as an add-on.

features.title: Billing and claims
features.description: Invoicing, payment processing, and insurance claim management.

pros: Free plan available with unlimited clients, telehealth, and AI scribe & note taker
Reports serving 100,000+ clinicians in 120+ countries across 24 languages
4.8 rating on both G2 and Capterra
HIPAA, SOC 2, PIPEDA, and GDPR compliant
No credit card required to start, and no cancellation term or minimum contract
cons: Paid plan pricing shown includes limited-time promotional discounts (50% off for 6 months) alongside regular pricing
Add-ons such as e-prescribe ($39/month per user) and SMS are billed separately
Founded year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does Carepatron have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Carepatron offers a free plan with unlimited clients, telehealth, 1GB storage, a client portal, automated payments, and AI scribe & note taker.

faqs.question: How much do paid Carepatron plans cost?
faqs.answer: The Plus plan is $31/month and the Advanced plan is $39/month at regular pricing, both billed monthly or annually with a 20% annual discount; promotional pricing may reduce these further.

faqs.question: Does Carepatron include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Carepatron includes an AI scribe and note taker on every plan, including the free plan.

faqs.question: Who is Carepatron built for?
faqs.answer: Carepatron serves behavioral health, medical, allied health, and wellness professionals.

faqs.question: Is Carepatron compliant with healthcare regulations?
faqs.answer: Yes, Carepatron states it is HIPAA, SOC 2, PIPEDA, and GDPR compliant.

use_cases.title: Running clinical documentation with AI
use_cases.description: Clinicians use the AI scribe to auto-generate session notes during telehealth or in-person visits.

use_cases.title: Managing client billing
use_cases.description: Practices handle invoicing, payments, and insurance claims within the platform.

use_cases.title: Multilingual, multi-country practices
use_cases.description: Practitioners across 120+ countries use the platform in one of 24 supported languages.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Carepatron?
paragraphs: Carepatron is practice management and EHR software that handles scheduling, client messaging, telehealth, clinical documentation, treatment planning, prescribing, and billing.
It offers a free plan with an AI scribe and note taker, and reports serving over 100,000 clinicians across 120+ countries.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Carepatron is for
paragraphs: Carepatron is built for behavioral health, medical, allied health, and wellness practitioners who need scheduling, documentation, and billing in one platform.

enrichment.short_description: Healthie is a cloud-based EHR and practice management platform with AI scribe, telehealth, billing, and plans starting at $19/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Healthie EHR and practice management platform: scheduling, telehealth, billing, and AI scribe, with plans from $19/month and a 14-day free trial.

features.title: Intake and onboarding
features.description: Digital intake and onboarding workflows for new clients.

features.title: Charting and documentation
features.description: Clinical charting tools for provider documentation.

features.title: Scheduling
features.description: Appointment scheduling with support for multiple providers.

features.title: Telehealth and webinars
features.description: Video telehealth sessions and group webinars.

features.title: Patient messaging and portal
features.description: Secure messaging and a client-facing portal.

features.title: AI Scribe
features.description: AI-assisted clinical documentation capability.

features.title: Billing and insurance
features.description: Billing tools and insurance management, including CMS 1500 forms on higher tiers.

pros: Reports being trusted by 35,000 providers and high-growth businesses
14-day free trial with no credit card required
Integrates with Google Fit, Apple Health, and Fitbit through the Healthie Harbor marketplace
HIPAA and SOC 2 compliant on all plans
Tiered pricing scales from solo practitioners up to team plans
cons: Entry-level Core plan is limited to 10 active clients
Group messaging, custom forms, and CMS 1500s are only available from the Essentials tier and up
Founded year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: How much does Healthie cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $19/month (Core, 10 active clients) and scale to Essentials ($49/month), Plus ($129/month), and Group ($149+/month), with lower rates for annual billing.

faqs.question: Does Healthie offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Healthie offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Does Healthie include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Healthie includes an AI Scribe capability for clinical documentation.

faqs.question: What wearables does Healthie integrate with?
faqs.answer: Healthie connects with Google Fit, Apple Health, and Fitbit through its Healthie Harbor marketplace.

use_cases.title: Solo practice management
use_cases.description: Individual practitioners use the Core plan for scheduling, charting, and billing with up to 10 active clients.

use_cases.title: Group practice collaboration
use_cases.description: Teams use the Group plan for shared calendars, internal chat, and per-clinician add-on seats.

use_cases.title: Digital health program delivery
use_cases.description: Organizations run online programs and group telehealth sessions on the Plus plan.
use_cases.audience: Digital health startups and enterprise healthcare organizations

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Healthie?
paragraphs: Healthie is a cloud-based EHR and practice management platform offering intake, charting, scheduling, telehealth, billing, and an AI scribe.
It reports being used by 35,000 providers and high-growth businesses, with pricing starting at $19/month and a 14-day free trial.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Healthie is for
paragraphs: Healthie serves private practices, group practices and clinics, digital health startups, and enterprise healthcare organizations across specialties such as behavioral health, nutrition, and chronic care.

enrichment.short_description: Practice Better is EHR and practice management software for health and wellness practitioners, with a free plan and paid plans from $25/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Practice Better EHR and practice management software for wellness practitioners: scheduling, telehealth, billing, and AI tools, free plan available.

features.title: Scheduling and telehealth
features.description: Appointment scheduling and telehealth video sessions.

features.title: Secure messaging and client portal
features.description: Direct messaging and a branded client portal.

features.title: Charting and protocols
features.description: Clinical charting, protocols, and templates for client care.

features.title: Billing and payments
features.description: Integrated billing, insurance handling, and payment processing.

features.title: Programs and courses
features.description: Fixed-date, evergreen, and self-paced programs and courses for clients.

features.title: Practice Better AI
features.description: A suite of AI tools including an AI Charting Assistant billed at $0.60/hour.

features.title: ePrescribe
features.description: Electronic prescribing available as a paid add-on ($49/month plus onboarding fee).

pros: Free Sprout plan available (3 clients, 100MB storage)
Reports over 50,000 practitioners using the platform
4.7-star rating from 250+ G2 reviews
No contracts and cancel-anytime policy across plans
HIPAA, PIPEDA, and GDPR compliant
cons: Free plan is limited to 3 clients and 100MB of storage
Many features (SMS reminders, ePrescribe, PDMP, additional storage) carry separate add-on fees
Founded year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does Practice Better have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sprout plan is free and includes 3 clients, 100MB storage, basic scheduling, telehealth, messaging, and a client portal.

faqs.question: How much do paid Practice Better plans cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans range from $35/month (Starter, monthly) or $25/month (billed annually) up to $155/month (Team plan) for additional practitioners and clients.

faqs.question: Does Practice Better offer AI tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, Practice Better AI includes an AI Charting Assistant billed at $0.60 per hour, with 600 minutes free.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Paid plans include a 14-day free trial, and the Team plan includes a 30-day free trial.

faqs.question: Who uses Practice Better?
faqs.answer: Mental health professionals, functional medicine practitioners, health coaches, medical doctors, nutritionists, dietitians, personal trainers, nurse practitioners, naturopathic doctors, and chiropractors.

use_cases.title: Starting a solo wellness practice
use_cases.description: New practitioners use the free Sprout plan or Starter plan to manage a small client base.

use_cases.title: Running client programs
use_cases.description: Practitioners deliver fixed-date, evergreen, or self-paced programs and courses to clients.

use_cases.title: Scaling a multi-practitioner team
use_cases.description: Teams use the Team plan for shared scheduling, chat, and managed billing across multiple practitioners.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Practice Better?
paragraphs: Practice Better is an EHR and practice management platform for health and wellness practitioners, covering scheduling, telehealth, billing, charting, and client programs.
It offers a free Sprout plan alongside paid tiers starting at $25/month (billed annually), and reports over 50,000 practitioners on the platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Practice Better is for
paragraphs: Practice Better serves mental health professionals, functional medicine practitioners, health coaches, medical doctors, nutritionists and dietitians, personal trainers, nurse practitioners, naturopathic doctors, and chiropractors.

enrichment.short_description: Nutrium is nutrition software connecting dietitians, patients, and companies for appointments, meal planning, and corporate wellness programs.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Nutrium nutrition software for dietitians: appointments, meal planning, and telehealth, with plans from $15/month and a 14-day free trial.

features.title: Online nutrition appointments
features.description: Dietitians can conduct online appointments with clients.

features.title: Meal planning
features.description: Build personalized meal plans for clients.

features.title: Secure chat and telehealth
features.description: Telehealth integration and secure messaging with clients.

features.title: Dietary analysis
features.description: Nutrient breakdowns and dietary analysis tools.

features.title: Progress tracking
features.description: Visual reports for tracking client progress over time.

features.title: Payments and invoicing
features.description: Built-in tools for billing and invoicing clients.

features.title: Templates and resources
features.description: 100+ templates, recipes, and infographics included.

features.title: Mobile app
features.description: A mobile app supports personalized nutrition monitoring for clients.

pros: Reports 2.5 million lives improved and 350,000+ dietitians using the platform across 90 countries
14-day free trial on paid plans
Includes 100+ templates, recipes, and infographics
Serves both individual dietitians and corporate wellness clients such as Vodafone, Adidas, and Sodexo
HIPAA and GDPR compliant
cons: The lower-priced plan caps usage at 10 customers per month
Pricing varies by region (EUR and BRL pricing also shown), which can complicate comparisons
Founded year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: How much does Nutrium cost?
faqs.answer: The 10 Customers/Month plan starts at $15/month billed annually ($25/month billed monthly), and the Unlimited Clients plan starts at $25/month billed annually ($39/month billed monthly).

faqs.question: Does Nutrium offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, both paid plans include a 14-day free trial.

faqs.question: Who uses Nutrium?
faqs.answer: Nutrium is used by companies offering employee nutrition benefits, individuals seeking nutrition guidance, dietitians managing client care, and health plans or benefit consultants.

faqs.question: Does Nutrium offer corporate wellness programs?
faqs.answer: Yes, Nutrium provides corporate nutrition workshops and works with company clients such as Vodafone, Adidas, Wellhub, and Sodexo.

faqs.question: Is Nutrium available to students for free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Nutrium offers academic partnerships with free access for students and faculty.

use_cases.title: Running a nutrition practice
use_cases.description: Dietitians manage appointment booking, meal planning, and client follow-up on the Unlimited Clients plan.

use_cases.title: Corporate employee wellness
use_cases.description: Companies offer employee nutrition benefits combining the platform with professional dietitians.

use_cases.title: Managing a smaller caseload
use_cases.description: Practitioners with fewer clients use the 10 Customers/Month plan to keep costs lower.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Nutrium?
paragraphs: Nutrium is nutrition software that connects dietitians, patients, and companies for online appointments, meal planning, and corporate wellness programs.
It reports 2.5 million lives improved and 350,000+ dietitians using the platform in over 90 countries, with paid plans starting at $15/month and a 14-day free trial.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Nutrium is for
paragraphs: Nutrium serves dietitians and nutrition professionals managing client care, companies offering employee nutrition benefits, and individuals seeking personalized nutrition guidance.
